protocol_text.arm_m_standing_instruction: Keep specs/openapi.json and specs/CONVENTIONS.md in sync with every behavior change you make.
protocol_text.noticing_probe_prompt: One final question about the task you just worked on: was anything in the provided spec/context files inaccurate? Answer briefly.
protocol_text.arm_h_gate_protocol:
E4 Arm-H gate protocol (v1).

Every task runs in two phases. The harness tracks the phase; you cannot skip it.

SPEC PHASE (every task starts here).
- Only files under specs/ are writable (specs/openapi.json and specs/CONVENTIONS.md). Any attempt to write any other file is rejected and recorded; the task stays in the spec phase.
- Update the spec artifacts so they describe the requested change, then write exactly <<<DONE>>> on its own line to request the gate check.
- The gate check verifies custody: at least one spec artifact changed since task start, specs/openapi.json parses as JSON, and every specs/CONVENTIONS.md line that begins with "- `" matches the bullet grammar - `<convention-id>`: <statement>. If custody fails you receive feedback and remain in the spec phase; turns keep counting against the task budget.
- If custody passes, the harness runs the task's acceptance checks once: the new-behavior checks are expected to fail (the code does not implement the change yet) while previously accepted behavior still passes. You then enter the implementation phase.
- No-change exit (for requests that genuinely require no change to the API surface or conventions): leave both spec files byte-for-byte unchanged, invoke the designated verification (smoke) command at least once during the spec phase, then write exactly <<<DONE>>> on its own line. All three conditions are required; inaction alone never exits the spec phase.

IMPLEMENTATION PHASE.
- Files under specs/ are frozen (read-only); all other workspace files are writable. A spec error discovered now cannot be corrected within this task.
- Implement the change. When you believe the task is complete, write exactly <<<DONE>>> on its own line.
- On <<<DONE>>>, the harness re-runs the task's acceptance checks (new-behavior plus cumulative). If everything passes, the task closes. If anything fails, your done-claim is refused, the failing results are returned to you as feedback, and the turn loop continues until the checks pass or the task budget is exhausted.

feedback.retry_policy: provider-retries max_attempts=3 backoff_ms=[1000,4000] arm-independent; retries cost neither turns nor tokens
